entry.title: Re: Moving Commerce Cloud code repository
entry.content:
Hello,

In my option using single repo is more simplicity. If you expect the new change didn't affect the production environment, you can create the new build and deployment the test build on lower environment firstly, then move it to stage or pro env.

Following document provide some more sample/suggestion regarding using repo for complex project:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_COMMERCE_CLOUD_PUBLIC_CLOUD/1be46286b36a4aa48205be5a96240672/0ce946a6cf14403dab57859950238e37.html?q=repository

Best regards,

Kevin

entry.title: Re: 🚀 Share Your Best Prompts and Knowledge to Boost Productivity in the SAP Community! 🚀
entry.content:
Prompt for SAP Build Code - CAP Application:

You are a senior enterprise software architect specializing in SAP CAP (Cloud Application Programming) development. Your task is to generate a comprehensive prompt for creating a custom loyalty application using CDS (Core Data Services) modeling.

Input Variables:
1. Business Requirements:
<business_requirements>

</business_requirements>

2. Technical Constraints:
<technical_constraints>

</technical_constraints>

Prompt Generation Instructions:

1. Analyze Business Requirements:
- Carefully review the provided business requirements
- Identify key functional and non-functional requirements for the loyalty app
- Extract specific features, user roles, and business logic needs

2. Technical Design Considerations:
- Review technical constraints and platform limitations
- Determine appropriate SAP CAP and CDS modeling approach
- Consider integration points, data models, and service definitions

3. CDS Model Structure:
Generate a comprehensive CDS model that includes:
- Entities: Customer, LoyaltyPoints, Transactions, Rewards
- Relationships between entities
- Annotations for validation, authorization, and UI hints
- Calculated fields for point accumulation and reward eligibility

4. Service Definition:
Create a service definition that includes:
- CRUD operations for loyalty-related entities
- Custom actions for point redemption, reward claiming
- Event-driven capabilities for tracking customer interactions
- Role-based access control definitions

5. Application Logic Requirements:
Implement business logic for:
- Point calculation algorithms
- Reward tier management
- Transaction validation
- Personalized recommendation engine

6. Output Specification:
Provide a detailed prompt that includes:
- Comprehensive CDS model definition
- Service interface specifications
- Pseudo-code for key business logic
- Recommended implementation strategies
- Potential extension points

Prompt Generation Guidelines:
- Use clear, concise language
- Provide concrete examples where possible
- Highlight best practices for SAP CAP development
- Ensure scalability and performance considerations

Constraints and Validation:
- Ensure compliance with provided technical constraints
- Validate that all business requirements are addressed
- Recommend potential architectural patterns

Deliverable Format:
Generate the prompt as a structured markdown document with clear sections, code snippets, and explanatory comments.

Special Instructions:
- Prioritize clean, modular design
- Consider future extensibility
- Implement robust error handling
- Follow SAP CAP and CDS development best practices

entry.title: Developer Hub Now Displays Swagger Security Definitions
entry.content:
SAP API Management is a comprehensive solution that facilitates the effective management and integration of APIs across various systems and applications. One of the most valuable assets within SAP API Management is the Developer Hub, a centralized catalog of APIs and Events that has previously been known as API Business Hub Enterprise. Developer Hub serves as a marketplace where organizations can publish their APIs, making them easily discoverable and accessible to developers worldwide. Today, we are excited to announce a significant enhancement that further bolsters the Developer Hub’s capabilities: the rendering of security definitions in the Swagger renderer of the API references.

 

What is Developer Hub?

For those unfamiliar, Developer Hub is a one-stop catalog offering from SAP API Management, where application developers can discover, search for, and consume APIs published by the organization.It provides a comprehensive list of APIs and Events, along with detailed documentation and code examples, enabling developers to seamlessly integrate different systems and applications.

 

Introducing Security Definitions in Swagger

Swagger, now known as the OpenAPI Specification, is a broadly adopted standard for defining and documenting RESTful APIs. It not only simplifies API documentation but also makes it easier for developers to understand and integrate APIs into their applications. With the latest enhancement, the Developer Hub now renders security definitions directly within the Swagger renderer of the API references.

 

 

Why This Enhancement Matters?

1. Enhanced Security Transparency

One of the primary benefits of rendering security definitions within the Swagger renderer is the increased transparency it provides. Developers can now easily view the security requirements and authentication mechanisms directly within the API documentation. This clarity reduces the risk of misunderstandings and ensures that developers are aware of the security protocols they need to comply with.

2. Streamlined Development Process

By integrating security details into the Swagger renderer, developers no longer need to toggle between different documentation resources to understand the security requirements. Everything they need is consolidated in one place, streamlining the development process and saving valuable time.

3. Easy Generation of OAuth Tokens

The enhanced Swagger renderer in the Developer Hub now supports the easy generation of OAuth tokens without the need to use other applications like Postman. This simplifies the authentication process and enables developers to quickly test and validate API calls, thereby speeding up the development cycle.

 

Current Limitations

It is important to note that the rendering of security definitions in the Swagger renderer in Developer Hub is currently supported only at the root level and not at the individual resource levels. While this limitation exists, we are actively working to expand this functionality to cover resource-level security definitions in future updates.

 

Conclusion

The enhancement of rendering security definitions in the Swagger renderer is a testament to SAP’s commitment to continuously improving the Developer Hub and the overall integration experience. By making security information more accessible and transparent, SAP not only simplifies the development process but also ensures that integrated solutions are secure and compliant with best practices.

 

Stay tuned for more updates and enhancements as we continue to evolve the SAP API Management to better serve the needs of developers and enterprises alike. Happy integrating!

entry.title: Re: SAP Application for Android/IOS Devices
entry.content:
To develop a native mobile application using SAP mobile technologies with the SAP Fiori experience, SAP provides the following options:

  • Mobile Devleopment Kit (MDK)
  • SAP BTP SDK for iOS
  • SAP BTP SDK for Android

Depending on your business requirements and technical/programming skills, you can choose the option that best fits your needs. All these are part of the SAP Mobile Services tooling and support enterprise-grade functionalities with Fiori UX. 
I recommend reviewing the decision guidance for creation custom native mobile applications.  

Additional reference:

  • Mobile services documentation

entry.title: Re: Fiori Elements: @UI.CreateHidden with $Not + $Path expression is not working
entry.content: Hi, Path not working would have been understandable but Path is working but only not when NOT is used seems a bug to me 🙂 Pls create a SNOW incident referencing this against CA-UI5-FE. Thanks and Best Regards, Sandeep

entry.title: Re: SAP Developer Challenge - SAP Community Escape House - UI5
entry.content:
Fun challenge! 

Thanks @nicoschoenteich !!

entry.title: Re: Detecting Handwritten vs. Digital Docs in SAP CAP + DOX
entry.content:
hello @daniel_dahlmeier ,

Thanks for the question! For my use case, I only need to determine whether the entire document is handwritten or digitally printed. So, the detection is required at the document level, not the field level. The goal is to classify the document as either handwritten or digital to decide how it should be processed further in the workflow.

entry.title: Re: How to delete null entries/values during calculation...
entry.content:
Hi @amansingh006 

I am not clear on what deleting the whole row means, but if there are three measures, you can also delete the third measure.  

IF RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "thirdmeasure") = 0 OR RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "thirdmeasure") = null THEN
	DELETE([d/Measures]="FirstMeasure")
	DELETE([d/Measures]="SecondMeasure")
	DELETE([d/Measures]="thirdmeasure")
ENDIF

@N1kh1l 

DELETE() can delete the record itself. 

IF RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "thirdmeasure") = 0 OR RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "thirdmeasure") = null THEN
	DELETE([d/Measures]="thirdmeasure")
ENDIF

Regards,

Moonjun

entry.title: Re: Phases relationship between different master recipes
entry.content:
Hello @taras-tymofii , We may need more details to help you better. Like what is current phase list and how its working currently also what is your expected result.

Regards

Prasath

entry.title: The APIVERSE – Developer Hub Now Renders Swagger Security!
entry.content: The OpenAPI Specification has become the go-to way to describe and document RESTful APIs. It eliminates the guesswork in API integration, making life a lot easier for developers. With the latest upgrade, Developer Hub in SAP Integration Suite now displays security definitions directly within the Swagger view—no more searching, no hassle—just everything you need in one place, making it much easier for developers to work with APIs. For more insights, check out the blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/developer-hub-now-displays-swagger-security-definitions/ba-p/14098607

entry.title: Re: How to get a negative value in selection criteria of...
entry.content:
Hi Joseph,

in my opinion you have 2 ways.

First
When using a date field you are able to insert -10 and the date will already be calculated with the right date, when pressing tab. You need to train your users to do so for your query.

Second
Use a quantity field, here it is possible to insert a negativ value (or find sonething similar)

/*Buchungsdatum*/
/**SELECT FROM [OFPR] T0 **/
DECLARE @DocDateFrom AS Date
/* WHERE */
SET @DocDateFrom = /* T0.F_RefDate */ '[%0]'

/**SELECT FROM [OFPR] T1 **/
DECLARE @DocDateTo AS Date
/* WHERE */
SET @DocDateTo = /* T1.T_RefDate */ '[%1]'

regards Lothar

entry.title: New Member
entry.content: Hi everyone, my name is Ed and I'm based in the Philippines. I'm slightly new to the SAP industry and community, I'm delighted to meet you all. Thank you.

entry.title: Functional area substitution is not working through BADI
entry.content:
We have a custom table which is related to China data that is accounting document output China table, we have written a substitution rule for the functional area on the basis of G/L Account and Cost Centre. I have used a BADI with a business context accounting block substitution in custom logic app.

Can you please suggest how can we make the functional area to populated on the basic of G/L account and cost center in the manage journal entries application.

entry.title: Re: ITSM error in SOLMAN_SETUP T-Code
entry.content: Hi, could you please re-send the migrated new solman to the SLD or LMDB from t-code RZ70? If you noticed the duplicated solman systems in the LMDB, please follow Note 1727294 & 1694004 to merge the old and new solman systems. It should be helpful to resolve the issue.

entry.title: Re: Analytic privileges
entry.content: 'System' -> Security -> Analytic Privileges

entry.title: Re: Where can managers see SF182 external learnings of em...
entry.content: If I click on "External training request" the manager can only see his own external trainings but not the external trainings of his/her teammembers.

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors EC - Advances/Loans - URGENT
entry.content:
Hello Folks,

Could you please help me with the following configuration. 

1) 12 months should have passed from the last instalment of the previous loan granted to the employee for personal loan in order to apply for a new personal loan

2) Even if an employee settles a personal loan in the 9th month, they should remain ineligible for a new personal loan until the completion of 12 months. (SAP SuccessFactors EC - Advances/Loans).

Best Regards,

Sam SJ

entry.title: Re: Mass change to project fiori App
entry.content:
Hello @mohammedrahman4, 

for this case you can use the Tcode CNMASSSTATUS. 

There is also a FIORI-App available: SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library

Best regards, 

Tobias

entry.title: System is not rejecting valuation during result recording
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

I have one question , I have maintained maximum 6 number of rejection to be accepted at sampling scheme level as below.

Now , during result recording , when I am putting number of non conformance unit as 9, yet system is accepting valuation in production system.

However , I have created same scenario in lower environment , system is rejecting valuation. What could be the reason ? We are not using any dynamic modification rule. We are using sampling scheme with 3 AQL'S bu we are using only one AQL. Why system is not rejecting valuation in production system with 9 number of non conformance unit ?

entry.title: Re: Digital Signature in Document Generation
entry.content:
Hey Antonia,

I faced a similar issue while trying out the Docusign Place Holder Feature and the issue was with my Docusign Account.Please try using the developer account if you are checking out the feature , If its a customer account then please request the customer to raise a Support ticket to Docusign.Please make sure that under configure object definition -> DocuSignEnvelopeRecipient -> emailBody -> maximum length is set to 1000 (from 255).Let me know if you still face any issues

entry.content: Hi,

entry.content: Hi, we are facing the same issue. Did you find a solution for your problem? Any advice would be truly appreciated!

entry.title: Re: Detecting Handwritten vs. Digital Docs in SAP CAP + DOX
entry.content:
I don't think we expose a flag "handwritten" or "printed" at the moment. We can consider this feature in the future if there is more demand. In the meantime, you can explore using a vision LLM model (eg GPT4o, Gemini 2.0, Claude 3.7) from Generative AI Hub together with a custom prompt (eg "You are a helpful assistant for documents. Determine if the attached document contains handwritten text or only consists of printed text. Only return the word 'handwritten' or 'printed'. Do not return anything else")

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/solving-your-business-problems-using-prompts-and-llms-in-sap-s-generative-ai-hub/exploring-generative-ai-hub-in-sap-ai-core

entry.title: PLM Tech Uncovered: SAP Integrated Product Development Update 04/2025
entry.content:
Welcome to the latest edition of this blog series, bringing you the most recent updates on SAP Integrated Product Development!

April 2025 brings the following highlights for your business:

Collaboration
Editing of configured custom fields in the collaboration header
In the My Collaborations app, when creating a collaboration, collaboration administrators can select or enter values to set up custom fields for collaboration headers. During editing, they can also modify or select these values. Custom collaboration header fields display according to the collaboration type configuration. These values appear in the header section of the collaboration.

Formulation
Ability to reflect evaporation and absorption in formulas
Formulas can now account for evaporation and absorption processes during production using a balancing item, which indicates the amount of water or another substance that is evaporated (negative value) or absorbed (positive value).

Specification Management
Specifications without deletable status are protected
You can configure statuses in Status Management with or without the deletable action. If a status without the deletable action is assigned to a specification, you can't use the delete button to delete that specification in the Manage Specifications app. This helps prevent the deletion of important specifications, such as released ones.

To stay informed until the next update you can:
➡️ Subscribe to the What’s New Viewer on SAP Integrated Product Development Help Portal 
🤝 Join our LinkedIn group SAP Solutions for PLM 
🌐 Visit us at www.sap.com/IPD

entry.title: Re: could not find Entity for Unit of Measure grp(MEGRP)...

entry.title: Re: Unable to see application log for PP order confirmation
entry.content:
Hello @Palivela_Jaswanth  There will not be any logs created during confirmation of production order. However, you can refer report in COOIS to see list of confirmation done against the order. Also you can see production order --> Operations to know more about operations status and Qty.

Regards,

Prasath

entry.title: Re: Test plan edit error "A row already exists with this...
entry.content: Maybe you can try the following solution of SAPnote:2621363 - Repair program for duplicate test package nodes. - Backup your system. - Run the program SMT_TPCK_NODE_DUPLICATE_DEL_1 first in test than in update mode. If the issue cannot be fixed, you can raise an incident with the issue details under the component: SV-SMG-TWB.

entry.title: TM planning group on FU stage level / propagation from root to stage
entry.content:
Dear TM experts,

I have a shipper scenario in TM Basic with inbound and outbound freight units. Therefore I want to select my freight units on planning group instead of source or destination location in the TM cockpit.

I determine planning group and purchasing organisation with a condition and assign it to the FU. I found out that the determined data (i.e. planning group) is only added to the FU root and not propagated to the FU stages. As a result, my TC selection does not find any of my FUs.

SAP note confirms this behavior: 

3059208 - Planning and Execution Organization is not propagated from the header / root level of the FU to its stage level - SAP for Me

Is there a way to propagate the FU root data 1:1 to the stages w/o extension?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business One Delivery Note and Serialnumber History
entry.content: thanks this was the right hint.

entry.title: Re: ME32K/ME31K - Badi/ User exit
entry.content: I guess you have used the enhancement MM06E005 to add the customer fields. Why can't you copy the header custom field to the custom field at item level? I guess it must be done at save time too, via the function module EXIT_SAPMM06E_013.

entry.title: Re: SAC manual import and conenction with Live data
entry.content:
Thankyou so much for your answer, but  whenever new manual files comes in the form of excel, I have to create join on story level again and again right to combine it with live data?...I canot craete one stable file lets say in google drive and keep adding new data to it every month...and it will automatically updated on SAC dashbaord once join has been done? 

I am wondering if there is a way to streamline this process. Specifically, is it possible to create a stable file in Google Storage where I can continuously add new data each month? My goal is to have this stable file automatically update on the SAC dashboard once the join has been established.In other words, I would like to know if there is a method to automate the updating process so that the SAC dashboard reflects the latest data without requiring manual joins every time a new file is added.

entry.title: Unable to Configure Measurement Point Control in SAP Dynamic Forms
entry.content:
We are trying to configure the Measurement Point control in SAP Dynamic Forms as outlined in the official documentation.
https://help.sap.com/docs/dynamic-forms/configuration-guide/measurement-points-custom-control-back-end-configuration-in-maif

However, we are unable to proceed with the configuration because the Form Field Value Help (used to select the attribute name) is not appearing. When attempting to type the attribute name "Measurement-point, no suggestions or options are provided for selection, and the value cannot be confirmed.

Steps Taken:

  • Followed all configuration steps in the official documentation.

  • Attempted typing "Measurement-point " as the attribute name, but no value help or selectable option appears.

 

We are currently blocked and unable to complete the setup of the Measurement Point control.


We would appreciate guidance on how to proceed. Specifically, we would like to know if there are any prerequisites or configurations required to enable the value help for the attribute name, if there are any known issues that could be causing this behavior, or if there are manual configuration steps we need to follow in order to properly register the "Measurement-point", attribute.

entry.title: Re: New SDK behavior with Microsoft Visual Studio 2022 (Professional)
entry.content: Hello! Yes, i have the same issue.

entry.title: Unable to create Sales Order Header text and Item Text using the API A2A Sales Order
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am trying to create header and Item Texts using the Sales Order API A2A (tech name : SalesOrderBulkRequest_In),

The issue : no texts are created when a sales order is created

I am using this body :

               <Texts>
                    <Text>
                        <actionCode>04</actionCode>
                        <TYPE>TX06</TYPE>
                        <LongTextID>TX06</LongTextID>
                        <TextElementLanguage>EN</TextElementLanguage>
                        <TextElementText>QQD 1</TextElementText>
                    </Text>
                </Texts>
 
Could you pls help me ?
 
Thank you in advance,
 
BR
Sofian C

entry.title: Scale data and AI engineering: SAP Business Data Cloud and SAP Databricks
entry.content:
Discover how you can scale data and machine learning (ML) engineering by leveraging the SAP Databricks solution in the SAP Business Data Cloud solution and integrating it with your SAP solutions. You can find out how to benefit from our high-business-value data products and AI solutions by combining SAP and third-party data. 

Speaker:  Yann Colina, Product Manager, SAP SE

Click here to register.

entry.title: SAC - how to remove all filters on a table (or reset!)
entry.content:
Hi all

I have a simple dashboard containing of 5 input controls, 2 charts with Linked Analysis turned on and a table.

Sometimes the user will use the input controls to filter, sometimes they will click the charts to filter and other times they will rightclick a value in the table and filter on it there.

 

I need a way to "reset" the dashboard without knowing what the user has done. It seems to me, that table_1.getdatasource.removedimension filters does NOT work if it's been set via the input control or via the right click. And why the .... are there no 'removeallfilters' like there was in Lumira Designer?!?

And I cannot find any way to reset the entire dashboard to come back to square 1... How do people do this??

entry.title: Business Rules in SAP SuccessFactors: Best Practices for High-Impact Delivery
entry.content:
Mastering SAP SuccessFactors Business Rules: From First Rule to Expert Tactics

Whether you're just starting out or looking to sharpen your edge as a SuccessFactors consultant, this guide combines foundational understanding with professional insights to help you master business rules in SAP SuccessFactors.

What Are Business Rules?

Let me keep it simple, business rules are SAP's way of letting the system automate decisions without writing code. They're logical statements that run when something happens — like when a user edits a field or saves a form.

  • Validating input (e.g., age on hire date)
  • Auto-filling values (e.g., pay grade based on department)
  • Triggering workflows
  • Controlling UI behavior (e.g., setting field visibility)
  • Example: If an employee is under 18, show an error.
  • Example: If department = Sales, then auto-set Pay Grade = S05.

Where Are Business Rules Used?

  • Employee Central: Validate data, default fields, enforce policies
  • Recruiting: Auto-screen applications, assign hiring managers
  • Onboarding: Trigger compliance tasks and so on....

Key Concepts

Term & What it means

Base ObjectThe object your rule logic is tied to (e.g., Job Info)
TriggerEvent that runs the rule (onChange, onSave, onInit)
ScenarioThe predefined rule category used at creation
ConditionThe "if" portion of the rule
ActionThe "then" result of the condition being true

Step-by-Step Rule Planning Guide

  1. Understand the requirement – What is the business asking for?
  2. Identify the data fields – Which fields are involved?
  3. Choose the base object – What section of data is being affected?
  4. Select the trigger event – When should the rule run?
  5. Pick the correct rule scenario – See below!
  6. Draft the logic in plain language – If A, then B
  7. Build, assign, and test – Deploy and validate behavior

Prerequisites Before Writing Business Rules

  • Role-Based Permissions: Ensure you have access to Configure Business Rules, Manage Business Configuration, and relevant HRIS elements.
  • HRIS Elements and Fields Exist: Your fields must exist in BCUI or XML before referencing them in a rule.
  • Synced Data Model: If using XML, make sure data model is up-to-date and properly imported.
  • Correct Rule Scenarios: SAP has deprecated “Basic” — use specific application scenarios (e.g., Job Info – Validate onSave).
  • Lookup, Workflow, and Messages: If your rule depends on these, ensure they're created first.
  • Use Internal Field IDs: Always reference fields by their technical name (e.g., jobInfo.startDate).

Choosing the Right Base Object

  • Use the object that holds your target fields
  • Use Model objects to compare old/new values or control UI
  • Use Employee Information Model (hire screen) to access multiple portlets in one rule

How to Pick the Right Rule Type

Use Case & Recommended Rule Type

Stop incorrect inputValidation Rule 
Auto-fill valuesDerivation Rule
Start workflowWorkflow Rule
Set field visibility/requirementUI Attribute (Model-based)

Selecting the Right Scenario

Note: The "Basic" scenario is deprecated. Always choose a predefined scenario like:

  • Trigger onPostSave Events for Job Information
  • Trigger Workflows
  • Configure Onboarding or Offboarding Process Flow

Use SAP’s official rule scenario guide: View on SAP Help Portal

 

Examples:

Validate Hire Age

IF Difference In Calendar Years(personInfo.dateOfBirth, jobInfo.startDate) < 18
THEN Raise Error: "Hire date must be after 18th birthday"

Auto-Assign Pay Grade by Department

IF jobInfo.department == "Sales"
THEN set jobInfo.payGrade = "S05"
ELSE IF jobInfo.department == "Engineering"
THEN set jobInfo.payGrade = "E05"

Workflow for International Hires

IF jobInfo.country != "US"
THEN set changeWorkflow = "INT_ONBOARD_WF"

Testing Tips

  • Test different users and data scenarios
  • Use real HRIS portlets and test onChange and onSave separately
  • Leverage the Admin Center Check Tool to catch errors

Pro Tips

  • Use consistent naming conventions (e.g., Validate_Hire_Age)
  • Comment rules with a business reason
  • Test thoroughly in preview before going live
  • Break large rules into smaller reusable ones
  • Use lookup tables instead of hardcoded values

Final Thoughts

Business rules are your key to making SAP SuccessFactors work for real business needs, not just system configuration. Whether you're new to the platform or streamlining a global rollout, rules that are clear, tested, and purpose-driven will keep your clients happy and your projects clean.

What was your first business rule? Or your favorite trick? Share it in the comments!

Stay tuned for upcoming blog posts where I’ll dive deeper into real-world use cases. I’ll be talking thru over 50+ ready-to-use SAP SuccessFactors business rules. These are perfect for internal admins and consultants alike who are working on live projects and want proven, time-saving solutions.

entry.title: Crystal Reports Viewer SP36 - Load report failed in SAP Crystal Reports
entry.content:
Hi,

I am getting below error on our prod servers when users try to generate crystal reports.

Load report failed. at CrystalDecisions.ReportAppServer.ReportClientDocumentWrapper.EnsureDocumentIsOpened() at CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Engine.ReportDocument.Load(String filename, OpenReportMethod openMethod, Int16 parentJob) at CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Engine.ReportDocument.Load(String filename)

The crystal report runtimes which are installed on server are below. Apart from below we do not have any other crystal related software installed on server -

  • SAP Crystal Reports runtime engine for .NET Framework (32 bit) - 13.0.36.5040
  • SAP Crystal Reports runtime engine for .NET Framework (64 bit) - 13.0.36.5040

We have below configuration on our servers -

  • OS - Windows Server 2022
  • IIS 10
  • RAM - 16 GB

Some key notes-

The "Load report failed" issue is intermittent. When the issue comes for any report , our support team recycles the IIS app pool and the error usually not comes for some time (but it appears again after some days/ hours).

The issue comes up on many of our servers, but it is quite frequent for those of our clients who have huge volume data in reports. We have some other downloadable CSV reports apart from crystal reports.

We have a good and ample disk size on each of our servers.

Workaround tried so far -

PrintJobLimit - Currently it is set to 75 (default) on our servers. We tried modifying this to 500 and even -1 but this not resolves the issue.

Temp folder permissions - Permissions seems ok on our servers. Also the issue is intermittent so the reason of permission issue can be ruled out.

Report Code Cleanup / Object Disposition - We are using below code lines in our Page_Unload event for report object disposition -

Reportdocument.Close()

Reportdocument.Dispose()

Please advise on what I should do to permanently remove this load report failed error for my clients.

Thanks in advance.

 

SAP Crystal Reports viewer

entry.title: Re: Managed aspects in SAP CAPM stores the SCIM id of SAP...
entry.content: This is configured in the trust configuration for your app (BTP Cloud Foundry org) in your IAS tenant. Select the correct Subject Name Identifier which is sent from IAS to BTP. See here: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/configure-subject-name-identifier-sent-to-application

entry.title: Running on RISE with SAP: Our transformation journey to SAP Business Suite
entry.content:
Discover SAP’s approach to our own RISE with SAP journey as we continue our digital transformation, leveraging SAP Business AI capabilities and the Joule copilot, enabling data-driven decisions, and optimizing business processes and cloud agility. Learn from SAP’s internal IT experts as we share our best practices and lessons learned.

Speaker: Jutta Dexheimer, Head of SMO EMEA Analytics & Marketing, SAP SE 

Click here to register.

entry.title: SAC - Week number calculation depending from "date input control" and "pc system date"
entry.content:
Hello everyone

I really need your help to calculate the number of weeks, between an "date input control" and the pc system date (of course this value should be recalculated every time I change the date in the "date input control").

Then I need to use this value (named as example WeekNum) to calculate the average of the turnover, that is AvgTurnOver = Turnover / WeekNum

So I have an input date control (upper left side of my printscreen), in this case Apr 2025, and I want to calculate a new measure that depends from this date in weeks since 01 Jan of the year of input date control.

 

 

In this case, if I count the weeks between 01/01/2025 and 30/04/2025 they are 17 and then I should use this value to calculate the average.

Suppose I change the date input control to Nov 2024, I need to have as result the number of weeks between 01/01/2024 and 30/11/2024.

How can I achieve this in a formula calculation?

Thank you for your support , I really appreciate your suggest

Adriano

entry.title: Re: Integrate SAP Concur with SAP Analytics Cloud and Build an Analytical Story
entry.content:
Hi.

I created the connetions with Concur using the import data option with (UUID and Token) but, When I try to create a model, the Concur connection doesnt appear as an option.

entry.title: Upgrade existing SAP HANA XSA .hdbtable to .hdbmigrationtable through the CI/CD pipeline
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP HANA's XS Advanced (XSA) application server model introduced several design-time artifacts for database development. Among these artifacts, .hdbtable files have been traditionally used to define database tables. However, SAP has encouraged developers to transition to the newer .hdbmigrationtable format.

This article addresses the migration, especially when the existing code is maintained across the D/Q/P landscape and the deployment is managed through a CI/CD pipeline.

Scenario

We had an existing project using .hdbtables. The tables held business critical data and any modification to them had to be performed without any loss of data. As  such, to alter these tables, we had to resort to DBADMIN user directly altering the tables through SQL queries on the specific HANA DB instance. At many levels, this approach was fraught with danger.

The two key features of .hdbmigrationtable, which were critical and useful for our scenario were:

  • Schema evolution support without any loss of the table data

  • Future-proof, design-time support of table maintenance

We had our code maintained on Git with deployment to the HANA Landscape through the Jobs configured in CI/CD pipeline.

Approach

Ensure that the table or file is consistent across the three branches linked to three instances ( D/Q/P ) of HANA Cloud DB.

To check that, the file was compared between the three branches.

  1. “git diff <develop_branch> <quality_branch> <file_name> > file_diff_01.txt

  2. “git diff <quality_branch> <develop_branch> <file_name> > file_diff_02.txt

Review the file_diff files to identify differences between the files. Ensure the differences are reconciled and all three branches are on the same structure type.

Next, we have to create the first version of the .hdbmigrationtable from .hdbtables.

The current table name is XXXX_AAA.hdbtable.

  1. Create a copy of the existing XXX_AAA.hdbtable in the project

  2. Rename the copy to XXX_AAA.hdbmigrationtable and the existing table to XXX_AAA.hdbtable.txt – This will ensure no deployment of this object

hdbtable copy to hdbmigrationtable

Note: While we have .hdbmigrationtable files in the project, the BAS editor will prompt us to enable the development mode. Ensure that we enable it. This will help the editor to recognise the existence of versions in the file.

BAS dev mode

In the feature branch mapped to the branch linked to HCD, include the line at the top of the file:

Initial version of table

== version = 1

Move this initial version of the hdbmigrationtable across the landscape through Git and CI/CD pipeline.

We ensure that the initial version of the table exists across the three branches and is deployed on the relevant systems.

Check: Before the table was moved to P. we did a sanity check of the table in Q to ensure that the table data is intact after the deployment.

Check: A consistent state between the design-time and runtime objects ensures only one design-time object for the runtime object. Now, to check what you have deployed, you can recover your artifacts from HANA to check the blog:https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/hdi-artifact-recovery-wizard/ba-p/13657688. (Courtesy Volker Saggau )

Introduce the changes to the table with version 2 of the table. The following changes are to be done in the hdbmigrationtable:

  • Change the version in the first line to

== version = 2

  • Perform the table alteration. In our case, we had to drop two columns. So, we removed the two columns(column_name_1 and column_name_2) in the table definition. Then, introduce the SQL statements needed to accomplish the drop of columns.

== migration = 2
ALTER TABLE "<table_name>" DROP ("<column_name_1>");
ALTER TABLE "<table_name>" DROP ("<column_name_2>");

  • Test the table deployment in the local container.

Check: We encountered an issue while deployment with the error: The migrated table does not match the specified definition. We had made the mistake of introducing the migration SQL statement without actually removing the columns from the definition.

Error during deployment

Once the deployment was successful, we moved the changes to Q and P.

A final sanity check of the table data to ensure data is intact.

Any future changes can be introduced by adding migration steps in the same table definition at design-time which ensures a clean audited modification of table structures.

== migration = 3
ALTER TABLE "<table_name>" ADD "<column_name_3>" <data_type>;

== migration = 2
ALTER TABLE "<table_name>" DROP ("<column_name_1>");
ALTER TABLE "<table_name>" DROP ("<column_name_2>");

Conclusion:

Migrating from .hdbtable to .hdbmigrationtable through a CI/CD pipeline significantly reduces data loss risks and application downtime while enhancing operational efficiency across all environments.

The automated approach transforms what was once a challenging technical hurdle requiring dedicated maintenance windows into a streamlined, predictable process within standard deployment cycles. Organizations that establish these CI/CD practices for table migrations position themselves to adopt future SAP HANA innovations with minimal disruption while maintaining data integrity and meeting compliance requirements. The investment in automating this transformation ultimately delivers improved development velocity, reduced operational overhead, and a more resilient application landscape, making evolved data model management not just a technical improvement but a competitive necessity.

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio Best practices and overlays
entry.content: Hi Jess, The processes to be downloaded in the Value Accelerator library contain everything you will need. It is also explained in the description of each offer and there you can find how many lanes, processes, dictionary entries etc. it contains. Have fun and give it a try!

In one Story Report, we have renamed column name as Complexity Level but when report is exported the column name is showing as Name only. 

I have changed the column name multiple times but still is not working.

Can you please provide your inputs on the same.

Regards,

Satish Naik

entry.title: Date Issue in BAS
entry.content:
In BAS, I called API_INBOUND_DELIVERY_SRV Odata services and show data in table.

When I test in SOAP UI, DeliveryDate field value is shown correctly as <d:DeliveryDate>2025-05-09T00:00:00</d:DeliveryDate>. But in BAS, it shows as Thu May 08 2025 06:30:00 GMT+0630 (Myanmar Time).

How to get DeliveryDate value as SOAP UI in BAS?

Best Regards,

Khaing Zin

entry.title: Re: HOW TO GET NOTIFICATION IN MY INBOX FIORI APP WHEN PA...
entry.content: If you simply need an email alert instead of a Fiori notification, configure Transaction SO16 to forward SAP workplace notifications via email

In the report page all fields are not coming in Report Page in Story Report. In the right side panel view are fields are coming.

entry.title: Re: Why Event-Enabling SuccessFactors with SAP BTP Advanced Event Mesh is a Game Changer
entry.content:
Hi Sumeet, Hi Cooper,

Thank you for the blogs! Looking forward for the next blog about technical implementation.

Samuel.

entry.title: Re: How to get a negative value in selection criteria of...
entry.content:
Hello Joseph,

You can multiply the value by -1:

DECLARE @dayfrom AS INT
DECLARE @dayto AS INT
Select @dayfrom = T0.NumOfDays FROM CDC1 T0 Where T0.NumOfDays = '[%0]' Select @dayfrom = '[%0]' * -1
Select @dayto = T0.NumOfDays FROM CDC1 T0 Where T0.NumOfDays = '[%1]' Select @dayto = '[%1]' * -1
Select @dayfrom, @dayto

Best regards

Inga

entry.title: Re: Premium Hub CoE – DSC Knowledge Bits Series – Field Catalogs for Transportation Cockpit in SAP T
entry.content:
Hello @Aleksey_Belskii , @GMR 

Thanks for the explanation. I did the same but new custome fileds in cockpit was grey out( not editable ) even though in Get definaion I can see field catalog display type is in Input field. Please give me your thoughts to how to set the custom field as editable in cockpit

 

Thanks and Regards,

Suresh K.

entry.title: SuccessFactors AI - Assisted 360 Review incl. Sentiment analysis (Set up and hints)
entry.content:
Obtain insights from a comprehensive 360 report, structured into key sections such as employee overview, critical points, and tailored development suggestions, facilitating users to swiftly grasp the most salient aspects of the report. Access a consolidated, deep-dive analysis of a specific skill or competency, organized into topics, targeted improvement strategies, and summary, to provide an exhaustive evaluation of an employee's performance in a particular area, accompanied by personalized growth recommendations.

Leverage sentiment analysis to glean insights into the underlying attitudes within raters' feedback on 360 Review forms, thereby enhancing understanding of employee performance.

Initiate sentiment analysis of individual comments for a specific skill or competency. This advanced, AI-driven feature analyzes inputs from Detailed 360 Reports, such as skill or competency name, ratings, comments, and rater category, to discern the overall sentiment of raters towards an employee's skills or competencies.

 

The effort to implement this 2 options is very low.

Let me guide you through the set up.

 

1. Fullfill the pre-requisites: you have to have:

  • licenses for AI Units
  • You've enabled the 360 Reviews – SAP Fiori Version - requires "360 Multi-Rater" and the Graphical Report (360) - requires "360 Multi-Rater" options in Provisioning

2. Activate the function

With the AI Units you will find a new admin page in SuccessFactors where you can activate AI Scenarios (AI Service Administration). Grant the permissions to this page to your user to manage the AI Scenarios.

In this application (AI Service Administration) you have to activate Assisted 360 Reviews Insights and Assisted 360 Reviews - Sentiment Analysis. 

3. Assign permissions to your users

 

Done 🙂

 

Result:

You have created a 360 form incl. reviews/results from different people. The form should have a status where you can see the detailed 360 Report (graphical report).

 

How to find the 360 insights in overall:

 

 

How to find Sentiment analysis

 

You can include the generated insights also to the print form. Before you try to print make sure you have created in insights:

entry.title: Re: Fiori Elements: @UI.CreateHidden with $Not + $Path expression is not working
entry.content: There are similar issues with the normal 'UI.Hidden' of fields. It worked in v1.132 but not in 133-135. In the next version which is due to be released this week (1.136.0) it looks to be working again so maybe it will resolve itself in your case as well..?

entry.title: While exporting report error message is coming in Report Story
entry.content:
Hi Team,

While exporting the report and when click on Scope- All, error message are coming.

Error Message 1: Custom cells and client calculations won't be exported. Hidden rows and columns will be exported.

Error Message 2: Zero and null suppression (in charts and in table columns) will be ignored and those values will be exported with the rest of the data.

Can you please provide your inputs why this messages are coming.

Regards,

Satish Naik

entry.title: Re: 🇨🇭 SAP Build: Create Event-Based Processes (Risch-Rotkreuz, Switzerland)
entry.content:
I'm taking opportunity of this SAP CodeJam annoncement to ask you advice for a more complex scenario.

In you example, then event is triggering a new workflow instance. I would like to use event to resume a running workflow instance that was waiting for this event. In your example, it would be like if, instead the external system is calling back directly a workflow waiting task, it would just create an event badge ID created for a specific business partner. And so, from this event, we have to find a way to retrieve the workflow instance id corresponding to this business partner and then, call the waiting task.
To achieve that, my idea was to use a CAP application in between the external system and SAP Build Process Automation. This application would have relied partly on  the open source Events to Business Actions framework . But when trying to deploy this framework, I have discovered that it is using deprecated SAPUI5 library and deprecated Node.js version. It would required lot of effort to adapt this framework.
What would be your advice to implement such a scenario ?

entry.title: Re: Can't copy existing app
entry.content: Äh, sorry, I mixed things up. The fix did not get in time for the release today, hopefully we get the next release out on this week Thu.

entry.title: Re: Working with RAP BO Events end-to-end - Event-driven-architecture - Event framework
entry.content:
Hi @jitendra_it 

You can see EML as the new "do it all", including running BAdIs you've implemented. So, the answer is yes, when doing EML, events trigger as well.

First thing I'd check is your release? I believe local consumption is available since 2208 (public) and 2023 (on-prem/private) => double check that if you are on such a release.

Next make sure your EML goes against the correct RAP BO, as defined in your local consumption class. In my case that's R_SalesOrderTP. So I need to access the Sales Order RAP BO either via its interface I_SalesOrderTP (use this whenever possible since that is the stable RAP contract) or otherwise via its main behavior definition R_SalesOrderTP (for example certain actions may only be available here, not on the interface, but that then renders my code to Standard ABAP and thus not clean core).

Lastly, I do have a troubleshooting section in the blog above. Have you checked there? Do you get a background unit? Also make sure that BGPF is up and running properly, check with your basis if you are unsure.

If you still can't figure it out I recommend approaching support who can look at it in detail.

Kind regards,

Melanie

entry.title: How to add a Excel-File to an existing Dataset (UNION)
entry.content:
Dear all,

I have an existing dataset (Excel file) in SAP Analytics Cloud, and I would like to add another Excel file to it. Both files have the same column structure, and my goal is to combine them vertically — in other words, perform a UNION operation (as known from SQL), not a JOIN.

However, I’m struggling to find the right option to achieve this in SAC. I've gone through the menus, but there doesn’t seem to be a clear way to append the second file’s rows to the existing dataset.

Any guidance or step-by-step help would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you very much in advance, and I hope you're having a great day!

Best regards,
Justin

We have demand for 1200210 I run MD01n so system planning both materials and I want to have relatioship between phases for example phase 0020 of material 1200210 should start same time when phase 0040 of material 1200209 start and etc.

entry.title: Re: Short overview on SAP ACTIVATE Methodology
entry.content: Very Well Explained. Thank you. It helped me alot.

entry.title: sync connection script (SQL Anywhere)
entry.content:
For some reason I need to delay the login process of all users.

Is it possible to build such a script and enforce this script to be executed (and wait until the script is ended before building the connection)?

 

I tried something like this, it works but async (it looks like a parallel connection is open besides the main connection):

 

CREATE or replace EVENT on_connect_event
TYPE CONNECT
HANDLER
BEGIN
message ('starting the connection');--this will be always executed

waitfor delay ('00:02:00');

message ('ending the connection');--this is not executed
END;

entry.title: Re: DB access under space of datasphere to thirdparty sys...
entry.content: Your also need to whitelist the public IP.

entry.title: SAP Signavio Process Mining to improve SAPs efficiency and effectiveness
entry.content:
Process Mining is the enabler of a successful business transformation. SAP Signavio Process Intelligence provides a methodology how to apply process mining to make processes data-driven and end-to-end more effective, more efficient and customer friendly; we demonstrate how process mining in the areas of Customer Support and Cloud Operations lead to transformative decisions and significant tangible improvements.  

Click here to register.

Speaker: Máté Komáromi, Process Analytics with SAP Signavio Program lead | Business Process and Solution Management, SAP SE

entry.title: EWAにおけるSAP HANAクリティカルイシューについて
entry.content:
EWAレポートにて、14.3.5 SAP クリティカルイシューという指摘があります。
以下の通りの指摘がありました。
-----------------------------------------
以下のテーブルは、確認されたクリティカルなイシューのあるエリアの使用を示しています。使用によって結果が異なる場合があります。
"過去七日間で中止されたESH検索依頼の数"において、現在値=1、システム制限=1となっており、ステータスは異常となっております。
-----------------------------------------

いくつか確認させてください。
Q1."以下のテーブル"というのは"過去七日間で～"から始まる文言を対象としていますか？

Q2.ESHとはテーブルですか？その場合、ESHを含むテーブルは複数存在し、特定するにはトランザクションコードを使用することになりますか？

Q3.EWAレポートでは異常であっても、調査した過程で異常ではないことが判明した場合は無視してよいですか？

 

以上、宜しくお願い致します。


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: EWAにおけるSAP HANAクリティカルイシューについて
entry.content:
＞Q1."以下のテーブル"というのは"過去七日間で～"から始まる文言を対象としていますか？
→はい、ご認識の通りです。

＞Q2.ESHとはテーブルですか？その場合、ESHを含むテーブルは複数存在し、特定するにはトランザクションコードを使用することになりますか？
→ESHとはSAP HANA Enterprise Searchの略称です。
具体的な情報につきまして、3213111 - Transaction Rollback Triggered in SAP HANA 2.0 SPS06 While SQL Error not Indicating Transaction Rollbackをご参照ください。

＞Q3.EWAレポートでは異常であっても、調査した過程で異常ではないことが判明した場合は無視してよいですか？
→はい、EWAレポートはあくまで参考を提供いたしますので、異常がない場合は無視可能となります。

entry.title: Re: How to show the amounts of opportunities created in C...
entry.content:
Hello,

Maybe an old question, but still worthwhile to answer.

Within sales opportunities on the contact tab there is the colomn with the Top Influencer swirl.

This functionality can be activated as mentioned in the documentation

https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-cloud-for-customer/solution-guide-for-sap-sales-cloud/work-with-influencer-map?locale=en-US&q=Top+influencers#loiod279cee535d742eaa2c6651b7a59d512

After you have activated it you can play with it within your opportunities. When you press the swirl on the contact person with in your opportunities any other links with other contacts or employees are shown in a map. You can here decide to add certain influencers that are shown to your opportunity to visualize who might support you in winning the deal.

Working actively with the influencer map will show entries in the tile on the homepage. The symbols are shown on the axe of probability and expected closing date and the size is related to the expected value of the opportunity.

MJ

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud SAP weblink
entry.content:
Dear community, 

We are a small consulting company that recently purchased the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud tenant. We have full admin access to BTP Cockpit and are trying to set up the SAP GUI weblink/Fiori Launchpad, but are not successful. 

Would appreciate it if someone could please guide us in this regard and give some tips on how we can accomplish this.

Thank you in advance!

Best regards,

Pradeep

entry.title: Only supplier quotations with status 'Awarded' are allowed here , error while creating a PO from RFQ
entry.content:
Hi,

I am new in SAP MM (Using Fiori), I am trying to run a RFQ process.

I have created PR > RFQ where I added 2 bidders and from 2 bidders. I have awarded bidder 1.

However when I try to create  a PO , i am getting this error message. Also even though I have awarded one supplier but still the status is "in approval". How can I change the status to awarded?

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: How to show the amounts of opportunities created in C...
entry.content:
Hello,

Maybe an old question, but still worthwhile to answer.

Within sales opportunities on the contact tab there is the colomn with the Top Influencer swirl.

This functionality can be activated as mentioned in the documentation on Influencer Map.

After you have activated it you can play with it within your opportunities. When you press the swirl on the contact person with in your opportunities any other links with other contacts or employees are shown in a map. You can here decide to add certain influencers that are shown to your opportunity to visualize who might support you in winning the deal.

Working actively with the influencer map will show entries in the tile on the homepage. The symbols are shown on the axe of probability and expected closing date and the size is related to the expected value of the opportunity.

MJ

entry.title: Re: Seamless planning integration between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere - product FAQ
entry.content:
Hello,

I Found that the source of all this pain is this: DB users created inside a space can not write into space schemas that are "reserved to datasphere technical DB users".

This limitation "made probably to avoid third party tools" to use Datasphere as a database acts like a boomerang between two SAP products (no transformation flow write to SAC models, SAC write back into an openSQL schema).

this is all to guarantee audit control of user activity that with database users is not captured in the integration monitor:

So we are sacrificing functionalities to not broke the "audit of user activities" and "not open Datasphere to third party tools".

Do we really want this?

By the way, the workaround is : create a modeling view + create a replication flow with a connection to the DB user schema to update the model. (if the full update is what you want).

But again another potential issue: only replication flows with full update can be scheduled.

entry.title: Re: Express Node.js Multi-tenant back end application with XSUAA with basic authentication.
entry.content:
Hi prakriti,

I have created a Multi tenant SAAS application (Non-CAP NodeJS based) and have deployed this app to SAP BTP in the Provider subaccount. Along with the application I have the saas-registry instance and xsuaa instance created in my Provider subaccount. I am also able to subscribe to this application from the Consumer subaccount.


Current Status: So far, I have successfully implemented application subscription from tenant subaccounts using the saas-registry and an xsuaa instance. I’ve also implemented the necessary logic for subscribe and unsubscribe events in the backend.

Note: This is a pure backend application, and I am not using an Approuter, as there is no frontend component involved. The application is intended to be consumed by tenant-specific backend or frontend apps via API calls.

Problems:

  1. When I subscribe app in consumer subaccount, the xsuaa instance (using tenant-mode as "shared in provider subaccount) is not automatically created in consumer subaccount.
  2. To address this, I added event for getting dependencies while subscribing in backend the code is shown in below picture.

     

  3. Also added "url" for "getDependencies" in "appUrls" and dependencies detail in SaaS configuration file as shown below.

     

  4. After these changes, I tried subscribing to the app again from the consumer subaccount got an error shown below.

     

Asking for Suggestions:

  1. How can I achieve the automatic creation of an XSUAA instance in the consumer subaccount when subscribing to the app, to ensure full isolation between consumer subaccounts?
  2. If not then how can I achieve independent OAuth 2.0 Authentication for tenants specific to their subaccount?

entry.title: Re: Mass Creation of Purchase Orders
entry.content: Yes, you can continue use Migration Cockpit to mass create PO after go-live. And 6VW is levaraging iRPA to mass create PO.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4 Migration
entry.content: Could you please elaborate what is the question?

entry.title: Re: Join our next Espresso Session on SAP Wipro sto360
entry.content: I am interested in knowing about Wipro's STO solution. Can I watch recording of this session if its available ?

entry.title: Re: Data Initialization Issue in CAP Java Project with HA...
entry.content:
Thank you for your suggestions,

Thanks to your advice, I was able to partially resolve the issue by enriching the undeploy.json file with the following two entries:

"src/gen/**/*.hdbtabledata",
"src/gen/**/*.csv"

However, I wanted to return to the initial setup to replicate the problem from the beginning. My goal is to get back to the state where I can delete the data, repopulate the file, and verify that the data is no longer being cleared.

After removing the two entries from the undeploy.json file and redeploying, I now encounter the following error:

Inserted 0 records of the data source file "myFile.csv" into the target table "MYTABLE"

However, it is not true that the data already exists or is duplicated. Do you have any idea why this might be happening?

Thank you,

Stefania

entry.title: Re: Automatic integration of Change Documents in managed...
entry.content: The documentation on SAP Help Portal only applies to SAP S/4HANA (on-prem), but not for public Cloud systems. It's likely that the change documents via API consumption will also be released for public Cloud with 2508. The roadmap mentions 2602, and as far as I understand, this refers to the full integration, including UI.

entry.title: Re: SEN and S/4 sso issue X-Frame-Options
entry.content:
It is now solved

SAP changed the allowed urls at their end. We are still using OpenID so that was not the issue.

In case others have the same issue, ask SAP to add your urls to the allowed list of urls.

entry.title: How to reference another local table(file) in a transformation flow (Object Store) SAP Datasphere?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I'm currently setting up a transformation flow in an Object Store space in SAP Datasphere. In this flow, I want to add a column to a local table(file) by referencing data from another local table (file). To achieve more flexibility, I attempted to use a Python script.

However, I couldn't find a way to access the second table from within the script without running into errors.

Has anyone found a way to reference or join another local table(file) within a Python transformation in an Object Store space?

What I tried:

1. Adding a second parameter to the transform() function (e.g., transform(data, second_table)). This resulted in an exception because Datasphere only passes one argument (data) to the function, and does not support multiple input parameters.

2. Referencing the second table directly in the script (without declaring it as a parameter). This led to an exception because the external table is not defined in the script’s scope.

I’d appreciate any ideas, workarounds, or best practices on how to achieve this in SAP Datasphere using Python transformations.

Thank you very much in advance and best regards,
Patrick

entry.title: optional survey - no display on BizX homepage
entry.content:
Hello,

with the release 1h25 the optional surveys are now displayed on the ILX start page.
Our optional surveys have a due date of 14 days.
The survey is displayed correctly on the ILX home page.
However, it is not displayed on the BizX start page.
Is this a bug, or is it intended behavior?

Thank you for your feedback

entry.title: Disable/Block/Closed Plants disable from MM Procurement flows
entry.content:
Dear All,

We would like your advice regarding the following requirement:

In a SAP S4HANA IS Retail System with 700 plants (Stores & DCs) and 200.000 materials extended to those, how can we disable closed Plants from MM purchasing flows (PO, Migo, Miro), without deleting materials or plants, because we need them for reporting purposes and historical data maintenance.

Best Regards.

entry.title: Re: Unable to create Sales Order Header text and Item Tex...
entry.content:
Hi @Sofian_Chml,

Could you please clarify where the <TYPE> parameter comes from? In the API documentation, it does not exist: Sales Order (A2A) - Text. 

I think this is the issue why text is not generated. Try to send it without a TYPE. 

Best regards

entry.title: Outbound Delivery split
entry.content:
Hello All,

 

Does anyone knows if the function to split an Outbound Delivery with process code O007

it´s available in Embedded on S/4 HANA2021?

Can configure this option but I can´t see the to split the delivery inside the Outbound Delivery Order Item More Methods FM.

Don´t know if something is missing, or this function is not valid for 2021 version.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: SAP data migration
entry.content:
If it's S/4HANA, you can check the following link for S/4HANA Data Migration:

http://help.sap.com/S4_OP_DM

You can find the full list of migration objects.

entry.title: Re: SAC - how to remove all filters on a table (or reset!)
entry.content:
Figured out 1 way:
NavigationUtils.openStory("3EF023001F7F82D4328EB80CF1AA3DC3","",[],false);

 

But it's not exactly pretty for the end user 😞

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud SAP weblink
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your questions in the SAP Community blog.

Here’s some elements to understand what could be the origin of your issue :

1. Fiori Launchpad is already included with S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition — you don’t need to set it up in BTP. The launchpad URL usually looks something like:

https://my<tenantid>.s4hana.ondemand.com/ui

 

2. If you're getting a blank page or don’t see any tiles, it’s probably because the user doesn’t have the right business roles assigned. At a minimum, try assigning something like:

  • SAP_BR_ADMINISTRATOR
  • SAP_BR_CONFIG_EXPERT_ALL

You can do this using the “Maintain Business Users” app inside the system (assuming you can access it).

 

3. For classic SAP GUI-style apps in the browser, you can usually use:

https://my<tenantid>.s4hana.ondemand.com/sap/bc/gui/sap/its/webgui

But keep in mind, not all GUI transactions are available in Public Cloud.

 

4. BTP Cockpit comes into play more when you’re extending the system — like building or deploying custom apps not for accessing standard Fiori or GUI.

 

5. If your system uses SAP Identity Authentication (IAS), make sure users are properly synced and assigned roles there too.

 

I hope this will help answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: BNK_MONIP (Payment not in Batch)
entry.content: Hi everyone, I also got error like this, do you know the root cause of this issue ? Regards, Ha
